figures.caption: Table 2: Quantitative evaluation on EventBench and NExT-QA. EC-RAG improves all open-source backbones on NExT-QA and overall EventBench accuracy, with the main gains coming from contextual and episodic reasoning Across overall metrics, EC-RAG enables all three open-source backbones to match or surpass the GPT-4o overall score on EventBench.

Table 3: System Ablation Study on EventBench (Contextual). Each component provides significant and indispensable performance gains in architecture integration, macroscopic causal reconstruction, and redundancy suppression.
Figure 1: Overview of the Event-Causal RAG framework.

Figure 4: VRAM consumption comparison during video processing. (a) The native 8B VLM accumulates KV-cache linearly, hitting a 32GB OOM wall at ∼162 seconds. (b) EC-RAG, bounded by a 12s maximum chunking strategy, plateaus at ∼17.6 GB, enabling infinite stream processing without memory explosion.

Figure 3: Qualitative pipeline of EC-RAG. (a) SES Graph Construction: Continuous video frames are parsed into alternating nodes of entity States (S) and Actions (E), compressing pixels into a discrete causal chain. (b) Dual-Store Retrieval and QA: For a complex causal query, pure vector retrieval only locates isolated semantic anchors (A1, A2, A3). EC-RAG utilizes these anchors to trigger a bidirectional graph traversal, reconstructing the complete chronological evidence chain required for accurate LLM generation.
